articles.title: What Prospective EV Owners Should Know Before Making the Switch
articles.description: Switching from a petrol or diesel car to an electric vehicle involves different technology and ownership mechanics. Range, charging infrastructure, and long-term battery performance are legitimate considerations that differ from conventional cars. Most of these concerns become manageable once buyers...

articles.title: Negri authorities investigate station owner for refusing to sell subsidised fuel
articles.description: SEREMBAN: A petrol station owner here is being probed for refusing to sell subsidised fuel despite having sufficient supply, says Negri Sembilan state Domestic Trade and Cost of Living director Muhammad Zahir Mazlan.

articles.title: 'Time to cut petrol, diesel...': Market veteran Ajay Bagga urge relief for Indian consumers
articles.description: While crude prices have largely returned to pre-conflict levels, fuel prices in India remain elevated. The hikes introduced to offset higher input costs during the period of geopolitical uncertainty are still in place, prompting demands for a reassessment of retail fuel rates.

articles.title: Donald Trump Jr. Says Oil Futures Under $70 Thanks To His Dad's Iran Deal: 'Lower Gas Prices Coming For A
articles.description: Donald Trump Jr. hails lower oil prices due to his father's efforts in ending US-Iran war, predicts relief for American drivers.

articles.title: Stocks to buy: Nagaraj Shetti recommends Ashok Leyland, Mahindra & Mahindra Financial shares to buy in the short-term
articles.description: The Indian stock market showed gains on Thursday, supported by declining crude oil prices. The Nifty 50 rose 0.70% and the BSE Sensex went up 0.68%. While realty and auto sectors performed well, metal and media stocks faced profit-booking pressure.

articles.title: Nikkei marks record closing high as AI shares rally on Micron forecast
articles.description: Tokyo's Nikkei index soared to a record high, surging over 4% on Thursday. This significant jump was fueled by a powerful rally in AI-related stocks, particularly chipmakers, following an optimistic earnings forecast from U.S. firm Micron. Investors eagerly bought back into technology shares, driving major companies like SoftBank Group, Advantest, and Tokyo Electron higher. Energy stocks, however, experienced a downturn as oil prices continued to fall.

articles.title: European shares gain as upbeat Micron forecast revives AI rally
articles.description: European ​shares rose on Thursday, led by gains in technology stocks, as strong forecasts from ‌Micron and Qualcomm assuaged concerns about ballooning valuations in the sector, while easing oil prices provided further support.

articles.title: European Market Surge Driven by Tech Stocks and AI Optimism
articles.description: European shares experienced a boost with technology stocks leading the way, fueled by strong forecasts from Micron and Qualcomm. The pan-European STOXX 600 index saw growth as chipmakers and semiconductor firms rose. Meanwhile, declines in oil prices supported investor confidence, while retail and airline sectors showed mixed results.

articles.title: Bears loosen grip on rupiah, rupee on lower oil prices, policy support: Reuters poll
articles.description: Short bets on the Indonesian rupiah and the Indian rupee retreated from multi-month peaks, ​a Reuters poll showed on Thursday, as normalising oil prices and central bank measures to attract foreign capital eased ‌some pressure off the currencies.
